key_insight: Increasing the barrel filling ratio from 43% to 53% made the detonation-sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr coating denser and more wear-resistant, although it also raised friction.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing detonation-spray filling ratio is a practical way to improve carbide-based protective coatings for high-wear industrial and high-temperature components.
method: Compared Cr3C2–NiCr coatings sprayed at 43% and 53% barrel filling ratio under the same conditions.
Characterized microstructure and phases using microscopy and X-ray diffraction.
Measured dry sliding tribology with ball-on-disc tests at 10 and 15 N and 5 and 10 cm/s.
evidence_signal: The 53% coating showed lower porosity, retained Cr3C2/NiCr with minor Cr7C3 formation, and achieved a minimum wear rate of 1.23 × 10^-4 mm3/(m·N).
watch_item: The higher-filling-ratio coating improved wear resistance but increased the coefficient of friction, so the best setting depends on whether low wear or low friction is the priority.
paper_title: Effect of Barrel Filling Ratio on the Microstructure, Phase Composition and Tribological Performance of Detonation-Sprayed Cr3C2–NiCr Coatings
